categories.name: Software development
categories.description: Skills and agents related to the fundamental phases of the software development lifecycle. These skills assist from the initial ideation down to code construction, ensuring robust creation.

subcategories.name: Functional & business analysis
subcategories.description: These skills help with identifying business needs, determining solutions to business problems, and drafting functional requirements before jumping into code.

subcategories.name: Coding
subcategories.description: These skills act as an assistant to help you write new code, refactor existing structures, and generally improve your software architecture.

subcategories.name: Software testing
subcategories.description: Focusing on quality assurance, these skills aid in generating and running tests, utilizing frameworks like Cypress or Playwright, and ensuring optimal coverage.

categories.name: Agentic use
categories.description: Specialized skills used for enhancing AI agents' capabilities, focusing on how agents interact with their underlying systems and memory.

subcategories.name: Tooling & setup
subcategories.description: These skills help configure agent environments, set up guardrails, or create necessary boilerplate structures so the agent can operate safely and functionally.

defaultCategoryDescription: Other assorted skills that don't fit into a specific predefined category.
